title: The Window Is Open. No One's Behind It.
strategy_topic: The small business owner who realized her website had a job — and it had never shown up for work
strategy_angle: Reframe the website as an employee with a single job: answer visitor questions. Most websites have never done that job once. Open with the uncomfortable question — what did your website actually do for visitors last night? No accusations, just a mirror held up to a silent homepage.
hook: The window is open. No one's behind it.

cta.text: Create your AI assistant — let your website finally answer when someone knocks.

voiceover_text: The window is open. No one's behind it. Every visitor who lands on your site after hours is standing right there — tapping the glass. And your website? It just stares back. Empty desk. Warm light. Zero answers. That warm light was always your website. It just never had anyone inside. Now it can. Your AI assistant answers the moment someone knocks.
video_concept: A professional services reception window — frosted glass, warm backlight, the institutional promise of presence — becomes a mirror for every website that signals help but delivers silence. Visitor hands tap the panel. It slides open. No one. Again. And again. The warm glow behind the glass was never a person — it was just the website, unattended. In the final beat, the AI assistant appears behind the glass: present, responsive, the promise finally kept.
video_script:
OPEN on extreme close-up of a frosted glass reception panel — warm amber light glows behind it. A visitor's hand reaches forward and taps. The panel slides open. Empty desk. A chair slightly pulled out. Dust mote in the beam. The hand retreats.

VO: 'The window is open. No one's behind it.'

CUT TO: a second hand taps. Same panel. Same warm light. Slides open. Same emptiness.

VO: 'Every visitor who lands on your site after hours is standing right there — tapping the glass. And your website? It just stares back.'

CUT TO: wide shot of the empty desk behind the glass — warm light from a screen on the desk, no papers, no pen, no sign of use.

VO: 'Empty desk. Warm light. Zero answers. That warm light was always your website. It just never had anyone inside.'

FINAL BEAT: The panel slides open one more time. Behind the glass — a soft, face-shaped ambient glow. A cursor blinks. A question comes through the microphone hole. An answer returns — immediately, clearly, completely.

VO: 'Now it can. Your AI assistant answers the moment someone knocks.'

FADE with soft CTA.

title: One tab closed. No record. No email. Just gone.
strategy_topic: Why hiring more staff doesn't fix the problem of visitors who leave before they ever make contact
strategy_angle: Challenge the instinct to solve the 24/7 support problem by adding headcount. More staff helps with calls and emails — it doesn't help the visitor who silently bounced from the services page at midnight. The gap isn't staffing. It's the website itself having no way to respond.
hook: One tab closed. No email. No missed call. No record. Just gone.

cta.text: Create your AI assistant — let your website answer the question that keeps your tab on the board.

voiceover_text: One tab closed. No email. No missed call. No record. Just gone. Someone was comparing you to three other businesses last night. They had a question. Your website said nothing. So they lifted your tab right off their shortlist. You checked your inbox this morning — quiet night, right? It wasn't quiet. Your website just had no way to answer the one question that would have kept you in the running. It still doesn't.
video_concept: A physical corkboard holds a row of card-stock browser tabs — each one a business shortlisted by a late-night researcher. One tab is slowly, deliberately lifted off the board. The gap it leaves is the entire story. We pivot to the business owner opening email the next morning: nothing. The gap remains. The video closes on that empty pin hole — a permanent, invisible record of a decision the business owner will never know happened.
video_script:
OPEN on a mid-toned corkboard in cool morning light. A neat row of muted card-stock tabs — each pinned in place, each representing a business on a shortlist. Three-quarter angle, layered depth, the row filling the upper two-thirds of the vertical frame.

VO: 'One tab closed. No email. No missed call. No record. Just gone.'

A hand enters frame — unhurried, deliberate — and lifts one tab cleanly from the row. The gap is immediate. The remaining tabs don't shift. The corkboard holds the absence perfectly.

VO: 'Someone was comparing you to three other businesses last night. They had a question. Your website said nothing.'

CUT TO: The researcher's side — a person at a surface late at night, three browser windows open on a monitor, one closing. Body language: focused, methodical, not frustrated. They move to the next tab.

VO: 'So they lifted your tab right off their shortlist.'

CUT BACK TO: The corkboard. Close on the gap. The pin is still there. The tab is not.

VO: 'You checked your inbox this morning — quiet night, right? It wasn't quiet.'

CUT TO: The business owner — morning light, checking a phone. Nothing in the inbox. A slight exhale. They set it down. Calm. Unaware.

VO: 'Your website just had no way to answer the one question that would have kept you in the running. It still doesn't.'

FINAL FRAME: The corkboard. The gap. One empty pin. No tab. No fill. No resolution. The gap is the ending.

video_script:
OPEN — extreme close-up. A printed analytics report slides across a pale eucalyptus wood surface. Fingers hold the edge. Camera tilts slowly down the rows: sessions, bounce rate, time on page. Each row has a number. Then — the final row. The label is there. The data field is white. Empty. Not a dash. Not a zero. Nothing.

VO: 'You track everything. Sessions. Bounce rate. Time on page. Every number has a row.'

BEAT 2 — a hand enters frame. A mechanical pencil circles the blank field. Deliberate. Slow. The circle sits there.

VO: 'But there's one row your analytics will never fill in on its own — visitors who left with a question you never knew they had.'

BEAT 3 — the report is turned over. The back is blank paper. Nothing written. Nothing tracked. A text overlay (burned in via subtitle): 'Not a bad number. An absent one.'

VO: 'Not a bad number. An absent one. You can't improve what you can't detect.'

BEAT 4 — resolution. A laptop on the same eucalyptus surface, screen open, showing a live chat conversation between a website visitor and an AI assistant. The blank field in the background is now out of focus. The conversation is in focus.

VO: 'Fenrik.chat gives your website a voice after hours — and starts filling in the blank.'

CLOSE — soft fade. Voiceover lands the CTA.

phases.delivery: Opening: empathetic frustration at the problem, not the viewer.
Body: conversational contrast — short sentences, not one long equally-paced paragraph.
Emphasis: slight lift on the key turn, contradiction, or insight line.
Brief pause before the reveal, punchline, or payoff.
Payoff: Slightly tense, intimate, as if the viewer is inside the choice.
Close: satisfying landing; CTA only if present, never aggressive.

delivery_arc.tts_instruction_fragment: Opening: empathetic frustration at the problem, not the viewer. Then settle into conversational body delivery. Use spoken rhythm: short sentences, contrast, and a brief pause before the reveal. Emphasize the turn or punchline — do not read every clause at the same energy. Avoid long equally-paced paragraphs; land one idea per breath. Slightly tense, intimate, as if the viewer is inside the choice.
